summary: Japanese surrealist/absurdist author (also known as Kimifusa Abe). Most commonly identified by his novel The Woman in the Dunes (featuring entomologist Niki Junpei trapped in a sand pit). Also known for The Face of Another, The Ark Sakura, Inter Ice Age 4, The Box Man, and his plays. Won the Akutagawa Prize for The Crime of S. Karuma. Collaborated with film director Hiroshi Teshigahara. Sometimes classified as magical realism.

works.name: The Woman in the Dunes (Suna no Onna)
works.description: His most famous novel. By far the most frequently clued work — appears in virtually every tossup and most bonuses as a key identifier.

works.name: The Face of Another (Tanin no kao)
works.description: Novel about identity and masks. The second most commonly clued novel.

works.name: The Ark Sakura
works.description: Novel about nuclear survival. Frequently clued in tossups.

works.name: Inter Ice Age 4
works.description: Science fiction novel. Frequently used as an identifier and has specific plot clues. Mentioned across 5+ questions.

works.name: Plays: Friends, The Man Who Turned into a Stick, Suitcase, The Cliff of Time
works.description: Abe's dramatic works. The Man Who Turned into a Stick, Suitcase, and The Cliff of Time form a trilogy about the stages of human life.

works.name: The Box Man
works.description: Novel about a man who wanders Tokyo wearing a cardboard box on his head.

works.name: Kangaroo Notebook
works.description: His final novel. Features surreal medical imagery.

works.name: The Crime of S. Karuma
works.description: Novella that won the Akutagawa Prize. Mentioned across 4+ questions with specific plot details.

works.name: Other Works and Minor Clues
works.description: Less frequently clued novels, stories, and biographical details.

works.name: General / Giveaway Identifiers
works.description: How Abe is most commonly identified at the end of tossups.
